leadText: Veluza (Japanese: ミガルーサ Migalusa) is a dual-type Water/Psychic Pokémon introduced in Generation IX. It is not known to evolve into or from any other Pokémon.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Veluza after shedding its own flesh Veluza is a fishlike Pokémon with a gray body; pointy , translucent, bright magenta fins; and red eyes. It has thin blue lines running the length of its body from its eyes, and its mouth is typically open, displaying several sharp teeth. Veluza's fins extend underneath its flesh, to the center of its body; inside is also a row of diamond-shaped spikes similar to a backbone on either side, with three of these spikes being longer and protruding out from Veluza's flesh. Veluza's main method of hunting comes from its ability to shed its own flesh, via its signature move Fillet Away . This boosts its offensive prowess and psychic power, as well as its agility, giving it an easier time when chasing down and tearing apart prey. It can grow back its lost flesh due to its excellent regenerative capabilities. The discarded flesh has a mild yet delicious flavor.

Generation IX Paldea #373 Kitakami #— Blueberry #— Lumiose #— Hyperspace #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Legends: Z-A . Scarlet When Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor. Violet Veluza has excellent regenerative capabilities. It sheds spare flesh from its body to boost its agility, then charges at its prey. Spin-off games Pokopia #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokopia . Veluza in the Paldea Pokédex Game locations [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation IX. Scarlet When Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor. Violet Veluza has excellent regenerative capabilities. It sheds spare flesh from its body to boost its agility, then charges at its prey. sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Gym Leader Kofu 's catchphrase is "Vaultin' Veluza!" Origin [ edit | edit source ] Veluza's name and appearance suggest inspiration from hakes , especially of the Merlucciidae family. Hake is an important fish in Spanish cuisine — Spain is the biggest consumer of hake in the world, accounting for around half of all hakes consumed in Europe alone. Name origin [ edit | edit source ] Veluza may be a combination of veloz (Spanish and Portuguese for fast) and merluza (Spanish and Portuguese for hake). Migalusa may be a combination of 身軽 migaru (nimble) and merluza .
